subject: Project Management
title: Scrum in Education
description: A 60-second lesson on Scrum in Education.

hook.text: A Dutch school replaced traditional teaching with Scrum. Students planned their own learning sprints.

buildup.text: Students set sprint goals, held daily standups, and ran retrospectives on their study habits.

discovery.text: Academic performance improved. Absenteeism dropped. Students reported feeling more in control.

twist.text: Jeff Sutherland himself championed 'eduScrum' — believing Scrum could fix education, not just software.

climax.text: Self-organizing teams of students learning to manage their own work — Scrum's most radical test.

punchline.text: If teenagers can run Scrum, so can your team.

quiz.question: What happened when a Dutch school used Scrum for education?
options: Student performance improved and absenteeism dropped
